book_summary: 1 Samuel is complete across chapters 1-31 in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book covers Hannah and Samuel, Shiloh's abusive priesthood, ark capture and return, royal extraction, Saul's rise and decline, destructive oaths, the Amalek genocide text, David's anointing, David and Goliath, Jonathan's covenant, Saul's violence, Michal's rescue, refugee life, Nob's massacre, wilderness pursuit, Abigail's prevention of bloodguilt, David's asylum and massacres from Ziklag, the endangered woman of En-dor, Ziklag's captives, the abandoned Egyptian worker, Besor's equal-share rule, Saul and Jonathan's deaths, body desecration, and Jabesh-gilead's burial and fast. Safeguards reject infertility shame, child coercion, clerical abuse, mental-health and disability stigma, ethnic hatred, authoritarianism, leader cults, oath abuse, genocide, child-soldier mythology, trophy violence, queer erasure, misogyny, domestic violence, coerced marriage, refugee contempt, collective punishment, witch-hunts, slavery apologetics, productivity hierarchy, suicide glamorization, and corpse desecration.

chapters.notes: Hannah's grief, prayer, vow, Samuel's birth and dedication.
Hannah's song; priestly theft, exploitation, and Eli's failed accountability.
Samuel's call, child-burdened disclosure, and prophetic recognition.
Ark weaponization, defeat, Eli's death, traumatic labor, and Ichabod.
Dagon, tumors, Philistine panic, protest, and cries.
Guilt offering, cow/cart test, ark return, and Beth-shemesh deaths.
Kiriath-jearim, Mizpah, Ebenezer, peace, and Samuel's circuit.
Corrupt judges, request for king, and royal extraction warning.
Saul's donkey search, servant guidance, seer, meal, and private word.
Anointing, signs, prophetic band, public lot, baggage hiding, and dissent.
Jabesh-gilead siege, eye-gouging threat, coercive summons, rescue, and clemency.
Samuel's accountability address, storm sign, reassurance, and warning.
Damaged regnal text, Philistine pressure, Gilgal offering, and weapon scarcity.
Jonathan's initiative, Saul's food oath, near-execution, and popular resistance.
Amalek genocide command, spoil, rejection, Divine regret, and Agag's dismemberment.
David's anointing, appearance/heart contrast, Saul's distress, and music relief.
David and Goliath traditions, youth, weapons, killing, beheading, pursuit, and plunder.
Jonathan's covenant, women's song, spear attacks, Merab/Michal, and genital trophies.
Murder order, broken oath, Michal's escape work, Ramah sanctuary, and exposure.
Danger test, reciprocal covenant, Saul's abuse, arrow signal, farewell, and grief.
Nob, holy bread, Doeg, Goliath's sword, Gath fear, and performed disorientation.
Adullam refugees, Moab refuge, unlawful-order refusal, Nob massacre, and Abiathar.
Keilah, conditional oracle, refugee departure, Jonathan's final meeting, and pursuit.
En-gedi cave, robe evidence, restraint, remorse, oath, and continued distance.
Nabal, David's planned household massacre, Abigail's intervention, marriage, and Michal.
Night camp, Abishai's proposal, spear/water evidence, confession, and separate paths.
Asylum under Achish, Ziklag, massacres, plunder, false reports, and witness killing.
En-dor woman, disguise, coercion, apparition, oracle, Saul's collapse, and meal.
Philistine coalition distrust, Achish's defense, ambiguous loyalty, and dismissal.
Ziklag captives, grief, Egyptian worker, negotiated safety, recovery, and Besor equity.
Gilboa deaths, suicide misuse, civilian flight, body desecration, burial, and fasting.

translation_notes: Fertility, infertility, pregnancy loss, childlessness, and parenthood do not measure human worth or Divine favor.
Priestly, prophetic, royal, and anointed status never removes material accountability or survivor boundaries.
Philistines and all named peoples remain human communities; ancient conflict labels may not become modern ethnic or religious slurs.
Illness, disability, body size, terror, altered states, trauma, seizures, neurodivergence, and mental distress are not signs of sin or demonic identity.
1 Samuel 8 warns what centralized power takes; Divine permission does not sanctify monarchy, nationalism, theocracy, or leader worship.
1 Samuel 15 is a genocide text. No living people may be identified as Amalek and no population may be targeted through it.
Jonathan and David's covenantal intimacy may not be erased; no single modern identity label should be coercively imposed.
Merab, Michal, Abigail, Ahinoam, captives, and other women are not property, rewards, snares, spoil, or transferable marriage assets.
David's refusal to assassinate Saul does not require reconciliation, restored trust, or unsafe proximity.
Abigail's intervention stops David's planned collective killing; her strategy is not blame for male violence.
David's asylum is real, but it does not excuse massacre, witness killing, plunder, or deception against third parties.
The En-dor woman is criminalized, deceived, endangered, and then feeds Saul; this chapter may not support witch-hunts or spirit-contact instructions.
The abandoned Egyptian worker is fed before interrogation and negotiates safety; the exhausted two hundred receive an equal share.
Saul's and the armor-bearer's deaths are tragedies under injury, fear, and coercive honor, never models for suicide.
Body stripping, beheading, and display are desecration; burial and lament restore dignity without erasing harm.
Mystical companion layers remain interpretive and never replace restored/source-audited text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Chapters 1-8 require continuing textual, child-safeguarding, medical-history, interfaith, animal-welfare, and political-theology audit.

Chapters 9-16 require continuing audit of Saul traditions, damaged regnal data, prophetic states, oaths and lots, the Amalek genocide text, Divine regret, Agag, David's anointing, and harmful-spirit language.

Chapters 17-24 require continuing audit of MT/Greek Goliath differences, Jonathan-David language, Merab/Michal traditions, genital trophies, Naioth, Nob, Doeg, Keilah, Ziph, and repeated Saul-sparing accounts.

Chapters 25-31 require maximum audit of Abigail/Nabal, the repeated sparing tradition, Ziklag chronology and massacres, En-dor terminology and coercion, Philistine coalition language, the Egyptian servant, Besor equity, Saul's death traditions, suicide misuse, Beth-shan display, and Jabesh burial.
next_actions: Begin 2 Samuel 1-8 with maximum anti-execution, anti-suicide-misuse, anti-queer-erasure, anti-war-glorification, anti-ableism, anti-leader-cult, anti-sacred-terror, anti-misogyny, and anti-imperial safeguards.
